transcript: Hey, my name’s Dempsey Brainard and, ah, I’m new to Athens, here.
I just moved down here from Santa Monica, California [clears throat] [delay & pitch]—
And I’m needin’ to move into a place ASAP. I got your number from the lady at the agency I was talking to.
I’m livin’ out of a moving truck right now.
And, uh, I need to get into something A-S-A-P, whether a storefront or a warehouse or a, you know, something like that. I think an apartment—
Okay, I think I’ll give you my son. H—he’s, uh—his number. Uh, you got something to write with?
My two boys, and they both have a lot of properties around town. They will—they will be over—
Benjam— [delay] Ah, Benjamin Barks. Okay. Now let’s see. I have a couple questions for ya: do you know, uh, is it okay— ‘Cause I’m about seven-foot-two! Are there any—
Is there any appropriate place for me to live, or…? [chuckles] [delay & pitch]
Oh, I’m sure they’ll find a place!
Also, um, is it ever—how you say—uh, acceptable…
…to pay rent, uh, once a year? ‘Cause I get paid but once a year! And s—
Uh, you can ask ‘em about that—
I don’t know— That’s my— Surely [unclear, perhaps, “they’ll be open to ideas”]—
You can arrange something. I imagine so.

Oh, dynamite! And I have, uh, three, four, uh, safes ‘cause I have some very valuable possessions—very dear to me—and I store ‘em in these safes. I got three or four of ‘em in there and they’re ‘bout, you know, a quarter ton each safe!—
…you have a [stammers] do you have a forklift that you—well how do you get ‘em—move ‘em around?
I do have a forklift, yeah. But it’s important that I’m on a floor level—on a ground level! These things’re a quarter ton each! [coughs] Each! [delay]—
Okay, I’m sure they—they can accommodate you!
Let’s see here. Now, anything else—I’m about 7’2”!—
So it’s important that I—that I—that I can—
Well, you’re a very impressive man! That’s a— I’d like to meet you! [chuckles]
Well, thank you! I would, too! Listen: you gonna give me a number? What’re you doin’ here?—
I am, yes. It’s for Benjamin Barks.
Benjamin Barks? Now, do I need to get—
Do I need to get him today and give him a deposit, or…?—
Uh, I’ll call him— I’ll call him up and tell him that you’re gonna call!
Okay! Now listen: I got a monkey runnin’ around somewhere. Now, do you guys allow monkeys, or…? [delay] Where do you stand on that?—
I don’t know ‘bout monkeys! Oh, you have to—
Well, he’s pretty clean most of the time. But he gets his hands dirty, you know, when he gets into the dumpster or the garbage can or… [chuckles] [delay & pitch-up]—
Yeah? Yeah? Ah, so, you have— You’ll have to be able to keep the monkey with you, huh?
This guy runs wild if you can understand what I’m sayin’! This guy—this guy is runnin’ buckwild! [delay & pitch]—
He doesn’t make any noise! No, sir! No, sir!—
Okay! Well, good! I don’t know—
…whether that’s a problem or not. But they will decide on that. Uh—
His name is Ewing, okay? And he is bonded with the State.
I don’t know what you mean by “bonded”.
Uh, the monkey is registered with the State of, ah, Georgia, here!—
Oh, yeah! [delay & pitch-down] So can we stop by and maybe set up a little conference, you and me and, ah, Ewing the monkey and just see how it gets—? [exhales] [delay & pitch-up]—
Oh, y—you can set it up with the—my son.
Okay. Okay, now where’s—? Now where’s h—?
Uh, the— Benjamin Barks. So let me give you his number.
[pitch-down] Okay, lemme get that number from ya. Hi, I need a pen. This pen doesn’t work, here—
Let’s see. And, um, let’s see. I’d like to set booby traps up. ‘Cause I don’t want—
…any intruders or invaders in my property. D’you guys allow that kinda thing in your properties or…? [delay & pitch-up]—
Oh, gosh! I don’t know ‘bout that! Seems, though, if it’s dangerous to people I don’t know
Oh okay. ‘Cause well, I need to protect my monkey up here. You know, I’m new to the area here! [delay & pitch-up]—
Yeah, okay! I’m sure they’ll be sympathetic to that!—
“7”— I got a pen now. Yes, sir.
“4752”. Now, you guys have Section 8 up ‘ere? [exhales] [delay & pitch-down]
I don’t know what a “Section 8” is.
It’s government-assisted housing. You know, I get paid by the government. But just once a year, now! [delay & pitch-down]
Oh, okay! I—I imagine they can work that out!

Excellent! Now, last thing, ah—ah, Cole: if I just dropped off Ewing while I go and meet with you son would that be okay? And you just look after him? He’s a nice little guy. Won’t cause ya trouble in the world. You just watch the monkey, I go meet with your son, and then I’ll come pick him up from ye. Would that be, uh, okay with you? ‘Cause it—
Uh, just go to—just go—go to the—my son’s house—
Oh! Come on! Just for about two hours. It should be okay. He won’t cause ya a problem in the world! Not a problem in the world! [delay & pitch-down]
I can’t do that. I can’t do that. I’m sorry.
Okay, well, then where am I supposed to put the guy? He’s livin’ in a moving truck right now. He needs to get out and stretch his legs. You understand. [delay & pitch-down]—
…uh, maybe, uh, Benjamin will have some t—suggestion on that.
I see. Okay, I see. Well then listen, I can— Lemme give ‘im a call and then we’ll see where we can—I see! I see! I see! [delay & pitch]—
[ LPC’s audio is continually layered until otherwise noted]
Okay, I’ll—I’ll—I’ll try to get ahold of him and see if he, uh, can, uh he w—he w—he waiting on you—
Oh! Oh, that sounds like a heck of a deal! You’ve got yourself one heck of a deal up ‘ere, Cole! And thanks—
…thanks for making time for me here today. You look after yourself, now, okay? And I’ll look forward to talkin’ to you soon!
Ah, now tell me your n—name again.
I’m Dempsey. Dempsey Brainard. I’m new in town!—
Dempsey Brainard. Ya sure did! You sure did!—
Okay, well that’s a little complicated, but, uh—
Oh, I’m sorry about it. “D-E-M-P-S-E-Y B-R-A-I-N-A-R-D”.
Now look after yourself and I’m gonna give your son a call and then we’ll meet up later today!—
I hope so. Uh, okay. Thanks for callin’, uh—
Thank you now, too! Thank you so much! [bassoon note]—
Thank you so very kindly! Look after yourself!—
Alright, bye-bye! Uh-huh! Yeah! Mhm! [bassoon notes]
Hey, yeah! [inhales] You don’t sound like Benjamin, huh? Anyway, a guy’s going to call you and his name is Dempsky Brainard.
He’s, ah, really tall. Like seven-foot-two.
I hadn’t seen him but he’s—he—he’s very c—conscious of it and—and that he might need, ah, special tending to find a place to live; he’s looking for a place to live.
He’s got—he’s got—he’s got a monkey.
Anyway, uh, I hate to give you this—
Uh, um, uh, but anyway—and he’s—he—he gets paid by the government—wanting to see— So he wants to somehow arrange that. He can— You probably know what he’s talking about.

No, I don’t know and I don’t wanna know. You’re doing a lot of harm to your soul. Because I’m praying to God you should drop dead, you know? So—and I’m a—and I’m a religious man! And I will, one of these days, tell you that you gonna drop dead because you—you interrogate me.
I “interrogate” you? Wh—who am I—who am I speaking to, please?
You motherfucker chickenshit! Piece of a—chickenshit! Criminal!
